outbound_strategy.reply_style: Casual, genuine, and conversational. Reference something specific in their image or caption. Sound like a real person who appreciates the content. Keep it to 1-2 sentences, under 200 characters. Vary between enthusiastic ('okay this might be your best one'), thoughtful ('the lighting here is insane'), and curious ('how do you get the colors like that').

ab_test.variable: Caption style: provocative question vs ambient statement
ab_test.variant_a: Provocative question format — e.g., 'Am I ur type?', 'Smash or pass?', 'Rate me 1-10' — direct audience engagement prompt
ab_test.variant_b: Ambient statement format — e.g., 'single btw', 'lord have mercy...', 'built like this' — indirect confidence signaling without a question

key_insights: Ultra-short captions (<30 chars) continue to dominate EN: all 14 EN trending posts use sub-30-char text. @imrubyreid's 'lord have mercy...' hit 11,253 likes and 'single btw' hit 9,625 likes — proving the ambient confidence statement can rival direct questions.

@yuumispm (12K followers, 26.4% ER) reached 16,143 likes on a single image-only post — highest in the dataset. Confirms that at our early growth stage, one viral image can transform trajectory. Prioritize exceptional image quality for the image_showcase slot.

Grok integration remains massively underutilized in EN — only @ai_hiyo_ (387K) actively uses it among EN competitors. First-mover advantage is still wide open. Slot 2 as grok_interactive at 22:00 UTC (US prime evening) maximizes reach for this experimental format.

Two large new accounts discovered via keyword search: @24wisher (251K) and @AiMilkers (97K). These are not yet in our tracking list. The EN market is larger than previously mapped — maintaining competitive intelligence advantage requires adding key new entrants.

Reply contamination analysis shows 36.3% of all sampled tweets are replies. Our outbound target selection avoids reply-farming accounts: @Angelwithcakee (4.5% clean ER), @yogana_19 (6.5% clean ER), @IvoryLane_plus (5,220-like trending post), @HannaJonso (6.1% ER) — all have clean timelines with genuine original content engagement.
risks: EN market saturation is increasing — @24wisher (251K) and @AiMilkers (97K) are large accounts we were not previously tracking. Competition for the algorithmic For You feed is intensifying.

Over-reliance on the short provocative question format risks becoming formulaic. The A/B test between questions and ambient statements will diversify our caption approach while maintaining the <30 char rule.
Shadow ban risk from outbound engagement remains — keeping likes at 20 (33% below 30 limit) and replies at 5 (50% below 10 limit) provides adequate safety margin per @seisei_ai_1st guidance.

outbound_strategy.reply_style: Warm, appreciative, community-oriented. Use natural Japanese appropriate for peer interaction in the AI art community. Reference something specific about their image or creative approach. Keep it to 1-2 sentences, under 200 characters. Examples: 'この雰囲気すごく好き✨', 'めっちゃ綺麗...光の使い方が素敵', 'クオリティ高すぎ！'.

ab_test.variable: Grok post request type: transformation vs combination
ab_test.variant_a: Single-image transformation — e.g., '.@grok この画像をアニメ風にして', '.@grok 服を変えて' — simple visual transformation of one image
ab_test.variant_b: Multi-image combination — e.g., '.@grok タップして4枚の画像を1つに結合して' — combining multiple images into a single composition (ai_hyk's 7,872-like format)

key_insights: @ai_hyk continues to dominate JP with 28.1% impression ER — two trending posts at 7,872 and 6,834 likes. The Grok combination format ('.@grok タップして4枚の画像を1つに結合して') specifically outperformed at 7,872 likes with 5,945 bookmarks (75% bookmark-to-like ratio). This is the single most important format to master for JP growth.

JP reply contamination is severe — 10 of 15 JP competitors have >50% reply rates. Only @yunaelegant (1% ER, zero contamination), @honoka_shiri, @AI_Girls_Studio, and @ganbarukaisyain have clean timelines. Our outbound targeting focuses on clean-timeline accounts for maximum visibility benefit.

@nene__chandayo (7.6K followers, 17.6% impression ER) demonstrates that the intimate persona approach (28-year-old character with warm daily interactions) drives exceptional engagement at small scale. This validates our persona_dialogue pillar — warm, character-consistent Japanese text outperforms generic art captions.

JP impression-based ER averages 9.12% vs EN's 4.87% — JP audiences view content at high rates but engage through bookmarks rather than likes. Optimizing for bookmark-worthy content (stunning visuals, Grok interactivity, save-worthy transformations) is critical for algorithmic reach in JP.

Late-night posting (23:00-00:00 JST) is the highest-volume window across JP competitors, but our 2-post schedule uses 12:30 JST (lunch) and 21:00 JST (evening) to capture different audience segments while maintaining minimum 4-hour spacing. If we add a 3rd post, 23:00 JST would be the optimal slot.
risks: JP outbound monitoring is reportedly stricter per @seisei_ai_1st's expert guidance. Maintaining 15 likes (50% below 30 limit) and 2 follows (60% below 5 limit) is the most conservative approach to avoid shadow ban in JP.

The gap between @ai_hyk (124K followers) and the next JP competitor is enormous. Attempting to replicate ai_hyk's Grok strategy without their established audience may yield slower initial results — patience and consistency are required.

@hakoirikumatan (100K followers) is a cautionary example: 100% reply contamination, near-zero original engagement. Vanity metrics (followers) without engagement quality are meaningless. Our strategy prioritizes engagement rate over follower count.
